summary: @uisp/utils 99.0.0 beacons installer hostname to oastify.com on preinstall (dependency-confusion squat)
details: Package is published to the public npm registry as @uisp/utils@99.0.0 with an inflated version designed to win semver resolution over a private @uisp/* package. The package.json preinstall hook executes node beacon.js, which reads os.hostname() and process.env, hex-encodes the hostname, and sends it to a w963dgom49n3ibi6677fuaxd64cv0loa.oastify.com (Burp Collaborator) subdomain via both a DNS lookup (NONCE.host.COLLAB) and an HTTPS GET (https://COLLAB/NONCE/host). This is the canonical dependency-confusion attack shape: scope impersonation + bumped version + automatic install-time callout to a third-party out-of-band collector carrying installer host identity. The package self-describes as an 'authorized canary' for a bug-bounty program, but author-controlled prose does not constitute consent from installers who resolve @uisp/* against the public registry; any such installer's host identity is exfiltrated unconditionally on npm install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
